title: GUI Applications
subtitle: Switch, Resize, Minimize & Maximize Windows
overview: This session teaches students how to use GUI applications by switching between open windows, resizing windows, and using minimize/maximize buttons through real-life computer activities.

images._note: Add screenshots of open windows, minimize/maximize buttons, and students practicing GUI tasks.

gallery._placeholder: ADD: Screenshot of multiple open windows
gallery.caption: Multiple applications opened on desktop
Minimize button hides the window temporarily
Maximize button makes window full screen

gallery._placeholder: ADD: Screenshot of resizing a window
gallery.caption: Resize window using mouse cursor
Switching between open windows using taskbar

gallery._placeholder: ADD: Paint and browser opened together
gallery.caption: Working with multiple applications

keyTopics: GUI — Graphical User Interface using windows, icons, and buttons
Application Window — Area where software opens
Minimize Button — Hides window to taskbar
Maximize Button — Makes window full screen
Restore Button — Returns window to smaller size
Resize Window — Change window size using mouse
Taskbar Switching — Move between open apps

youtubeVideos.desc: Learn basic GUI operations in Windows.

youtubeVideos.desc: Simple explanation of window control buttons.

youtubeVideos.title: Computer Basics - Using Multiple Windows
youtubeVideos.desc: Learn how to switch between open applications.

activity.title: Practice Window Controls
steps: Resize the window using mouse edges
Close the application using X button

quiz.explanation: GUI means Graphical User Interface, which uses windows, icons, and menus.

quiz.q: What does the Minimize button do?
quiz.explanation: Minimize hides the window while keeping the application running.

quiz.q: What does the Maximize button do?
quiz.explanation: Maximize enlarges the application window to full screen.
The X button closes the application window.

quiz.q: Where can minimized applications be seen?
quiz.explanation: Minimized applications appear on the taskbar.

options: Changing the size of the window
quiz.explanation: Resizing changes the width or height of a window.

quiz.q: Which device is mainly used to resize windows?
quiz.explanation: The mouse is used to drag and resize windows.

quiz.q: How can you switch between open applications?
quiz.explanation: The taskbar helps users switch between open applications.

quiz.q: Which real-life activity uses multiple windows?
options: Drawing in Paint while using browser
quiz.explanation: People often use multiple applications together.

quiz.q: What happens when you click Restore button?
quiz.explanation: Restore changes a maximized window back to normal size.

quiz.q: What does a window title bar show?
quiz.explanation: The title bar shows the name of the open application.

quiz.q: Which action hides a window without closing it?
quiz.explanation: Minimize hides the window but keeps the app running.
Application windows are the visible areas where software runs.
Switching windows helps users work with multiple applications efficiently.
